lobby.name: Resort Lobby
lobby.description: The Grand Palm Resort lobby. Dust sheets drape the furniture and a chandelier sways with no breeze. Mr. Crane, the caretaker, hovers behind the front desk.

navigation_links.label: Janitor's Closet
navigation_links.locked_message: The janitor's closet door is locked tight. A heavy old lock — you'd need the right key.

navigation_links.label: Manager's Office
navigation_links.locked_message: The manager's office at the top of the stairs is locked. Mr. Crane 'keeps the only key'... though Gus might know otherwise.

clickable_hotspots.label: Locked Closet Door
clickable_hotspots.missing_item_message: The janitor's closet. The lock is old but sturdy — no key, no entry.

action_triggers.text: The key turns with a rusty CLUNK. The janitor's closet is open! Head west to look inside.
Inside the drawer: a heavy old flashlight. It still works! Added to inventory.

clickable_hotspots.label: Guest Ledger
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: Rows of cramped, faded bookkeeping. Deciphering this is a job for Velma.

action_triggers.text: Velma: 'Jinkies! According to this ledger, the resort is drowning in debt... and someone's been billing supplies to a room that doesn't exist.'

decoy_costume.message: You unfurl the counterfeit phantom. Crane doesn't scream — he INSPECTS it. 'The hem is wrong,' he sniffs... then freezes. Velma, sweetly: 'Wrong compared to WHAT, Mr. Crane?'

muddy_glove.message: You dangle the muddy glove. Crane's hand twitches toward his jacket pocket — where its TWIN is peeking out. 'Never seen it. Gloves are... common.' His left hand, notably, is bare.

clickable_hotspots.label: Faded Poster
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A sun-bleached poster with something scrawled across it. Threatening graffiti is Shaggy and Scooby's area of expertise — mostly the running-away part.

action_triggers.text: Shaggy reads the scrawl aloud: 'THE PHANTOM GUEST CHECKS IN, YOU CHECK OUT.' Scooby sniffs it and recoils — 'Rarker!' Fresh marker ink. Like, fifty-year-old ghosts don't buy NEW markers, man!
Tucked in a dusty suitcase: a 1969 grand-opening brochure. Founder George Palm Sr. built this place 'for my family, forever.' Velma: 'So who inherits a resort nobody will buy...?'

clickable_hotspots.label: Office Stairs (locked door)
clickable_hotspots.missing_item_message: A brass-railed staircase climbs to the manager's office. The door at the top is locked tight.

action_triggers.text: Gus's passkey fits! The office door creaks open at the top of the stairs. Head north.

clickable_hotspots.label: Dust-Sheeted Armchair
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A hulking armchair under a dust sheet, wedged hard against the wall. Shoving heavy suspicious furniture is Freddy's whole personality.

action_triggers.text: Fred yanks off the sheet and walks the armchair aside — fresh DRAG TRACKS score the dust underneath, arrow-straight toward the office stairs. 'Somebody rearranges this lobby after hours, gang. Furniture doesn't moonwalk.'

clickable_hotspots.label: Swaying Chandelier
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: The chandelier sways with NO breeze. Somebody with an eye for stagecraft should study it — where's Daphne?

action_triggers.text: Daphne squints up: 'FISHING LINE. The chandelier's rigged to a pull-wire that runs into the wall. Our phantom does his own special effects... on a community-theater budget.'

grounds.name: Resort Grounds
grounds.description: Overgrown gardens surround the drained pool. The dark toolshed looms north, a glass greenhouse glimmers south, and a vine-choked archway leads east. Janitor Gus rakes the same spot of dead leaves, forever.

navigation_links.label: Toolshed
navigation_links.locked_message: The toolshed is pitch dark inside. Light it up first — a flashlight on that doorway would do.

navigation_links.label: Pool Cabana
navigation_links.locked_message: The archway east to the pool deck is choked with vines. You'll need something sharp to clear it.

navigation_links.label: Pump Room
navigation_links.locked_message: The pool's maintenance hatch is cranked shut. No handle, no entry.

action_triggers.text: You fish a long, sturdy stick out of the bushes. You never know when a good stick will come in handy.

decoy_costume.message: You raise the glowing sheet. Gus levels his rake like a lance: 'BACK, HAUNT!' ...then squints closer. 'That's paint and a bedsheet, ain't it.' He lowers the rake, thoroughly disgusted. 'Congratulations, kids. You built a SECOND ghost.'

clickable_hotspots.label: Dark Toolshed
clickable_hotspots.missing_item_message: The toolshed doorway is pitch black. Without a light you'd trip over every rake in Florida. (Velma has the flashlight... if you found it.)

action_triggers.text: The flashlight beam floods the shed — cobwebbed tools, a workbench... and something GLINTING under a floor grate inside. You can head NORTH into the toolshed now.

clickable_hotspots.label: Drained Pool
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: Fresh footprints crisscross the leaf muck at the bottom of the drained pool. Reading a trail like a crime scene is Velma's kind of fun.

action_triggers.text: Velma crouches at the rim: 'Size 12 sneakers, even stride, no drifting — our phantom WALKS his haunting route, and he always exits toward the lobby. Ghosts float, gentlemen. Employees commute.'

clickable_hotspots.label: Something in the Pool Muck
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: Something pale is half-buried in the pool muck. Identifying designer anything at twenty paces is Daphne's superpower — radio her in.

action_triggers.text: Half-buried in the leaf muck: a dress glove, monogrammed 'A.C.'. Daphne: 'Ambrose Crane wears gloves JUST like these. Ghosts don't do monograms.'

clickable_hotspots.label: Overgrown Archway
clickable_hotspots.missing_item_message: A stone archway to the pool deck — completely sealed by a decade of vines. You'd need serious garden shears to cut through. Maybe the greenhouse (south) has some.

action_triggers.text: SNIP-SNIP-SNIP! Daphne directs, everyone else ducks flying vine bits. The archway to the POOL CABANA is clear — head east!

clickable_hotspots.label: Pool Maintenance Hatch
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A rusted steel hatch stamped 'PUMP ROOM'. Even with a crank, winding open a decade of rust is a job for team muscle — get Fred on the walkie.
clickable_hotspots.missing_item_message: A rusted steel hatch in the pool floor, stamped 'PUMP ROOM'. The crank socket is empty — the handle must be stored with the tools somewhere.

action_triggers.text: The crank fits! Fred winds it and the hatch grinds open, breathing out cold, chlorine-and-mildew air. A ladder drops DOWN into the dark under the resort. Shaggy: 'I vote we never.'

clickable_hotspots.label: Wild Mushrooms
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A clump of fat wild mushrooms by the shed. Only two members of this gang consider lawn fungus 'a snack situation'.

action_triggers.text: Scooby hoovers up a mushroom before Shaggy can yell 'POISON!' ...It's fine. Probably. Between caps, Shaggy spots a line of CRUSHED mushrooms — somebody heavy tromped toward the shed, recently. 'Like, ghosts FLOAT, man. This dude STOMPS.'

janitor_closet.name: Janitor's Closet
janitor_closet.description: A cramped closet reeking of bleach and something... chemical. Shelves sag with supplies, a coil of rope hangs on the wall, and a padlocked grate is set into the floor.

navigation_links.label: Boiler Room
navigation_links.locked_message: A padlocked grate blocks the way down. The lock is rusted but stubborn.

clickable_hotspots.label: Shadowy Back Shelf
clickable_hotspots.missing_item_message: The back shelves are lost in shadow. You can make out cans of... something. Too dark to read the labels.

action_triggers.text: The beam lands on a half-empty can of LUMO-GLOW PHANTOM GREEN theatrical paint — the exact eerie green of the Phantom Guest! You take the can as evidence.
A long coil of sturdy rope. Freddy would be proud — this is prime trap-building material.

clickable_hotspots.label: Padlocked Floor Grate
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A rusty padlock seals the floor grate. Warm air and a faint green glow seep up from below. Picking locks is Daphne's department.

action_triggers.text: Daphne slides a hairpin from her hair and works the padlock... click... CLICK! 'And Freddy says I'm danger-prone.' The way down to the boiler room is open!

clickable_hotspots.label: Crumpled Invoice
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A crumpled invoice covered in smudged fine print. Deciphering supplier paperwork is Velma's department.

action_triggers.text: Velma smooths the invoice: '6 cans LUMO-GLOW paint — CHARGED TO ROOM 217.' She pushes up her glasses. 'The ledger says there IS no Room 217. Somebody's hiding purchases inside a fake room number.'
A box cutter on the shelf edge. Handy for opening crates — or cutting somebody loose.

clickable_hotspots.label: Dented Supply Cabinet
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A dented steel supply cabinet, wedged crooked against the wall. Heavy-appliance choreography is a Fred event.

action_triggers.text: Fred walks the cabinet aside in three grunts. Scratched into the wall behind it: a stash hollow, EMPTY except for fresh paint-can rings. 'Somebody used this closet as a supply drop long before we showed up, gang.'

clickable_hotspots.label: Mop Bucket Corner
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A mop bucket that smells like bleach and regret. Snout-first investigation is the Shaggy & Scooby specialty.

action_triggers.text: Scooby noses the mop bucket — SNEEZE — and out rolls a crumpled BURGER WRAPPER and a soda cup, still cold. Shaggy: 'Zoinks, the phantom eats FAST FOOD? Like... same, but SUSPICIOUS.'

trap_room.name: Boiler Room (Trap Room)
trap_room.description: The boiler room glows sickly green — glowing footprints everywhere! A bedsheet 'costume' hangs by the furnace. An iron hook juts from a ceiling beam above a bait-sized crate, next to a big red lever marked 'LAUNDRY NET'.

clickable_hotspots.label: Iron Ceiling Hook
clickable_hotspots.missing_item_message: A sturdy iron hook juts from the beam, right above the crate. Perfect for rigging a net trap... if you had a rope.

action_triggers.text: Fred whips the rope into a double-pulley snare net like he's done it a hundred times. (He has.) 'Classic Palm Beach Snare, gang. When the phantom takes the bait — WHOOSH.'

clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: An iron hook, a coil of rope, a phantom to catch... this is a job for Freddy 'The Trap' Jones. Radio him in!

clickable_hotspots.label: Bait Crate
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: The crate sits right under the net. It needs bait — and nobody arranges a Scooby Snack more irresistibly than Shaggy and Scooby.
clickable_hotspots.missing_item_message: The crate sits right under the net. It needs bait. Something no phantom with taste could resist... like a Scooby Snack.

action_triggers.text: Shaggy balances the Scooby Snack on the crate at the perfect tempting angle. Scooby whimpers: 'Rye rye, rittle ruddy...' The trap is baited!

clickable_hotspots.label: Glowing Bedsheet
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A glowing bedsheet 'costume' hangs by the furnace. One of us can read fabric like a fingerprint — get Daphne on the walkie.

action_triggers.text: Daphne pinches the hem at arm's length: 'Still WET. And this stitching matches a hurried hand — same thread as the sheets someone's been sewing. He'll be back for his costume soon... better spring the trap before then!'

clickable_hotspots.label: Boiler Pressure Gauge
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: The boiler's pressure gauge needle is drifting lazily upward. Interpreting dials is peak Velma content.

action_triggers.text: Velma taps the gauge: 'Pressure's UP. This boiler ran within the HOUR — in a resort that's been "abandoned" for years. Heating bills are murder... unless you're planning to own the place by Friday.'

clickable_hotspots.label: Red Lever — 'LAUNDRY NET'
clickable_hotspots.locked_message: Not yet! To spring the trap you need the net rigged, the bait set, Gus's testimony about the paint — and you'd better find whoever the phantom has been HIDING before you tip your hand. (Crane's office might hold answers.)

office.name: Manager's Office
office.description: Mr. Crane's office. Founder George Palm Sr. glares from a portrait above a wall safe. A blueprint chest, a stuffed bookshelf, and a desk drowning in paperwork.

navigation_links.label: Room 217
navigation_links.locked_message: The east wall is solid bookshelf... though the blueprint suggests otherwise.

clickable_hotspots.label: Blueprint Chest
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A flat chest of yellowed blueprints. Deciphering 1960s architecture is Velma's kind of fun.

action_triggers.text: Velma unrolls the master blueprint: 'Jinkies! There IS a Room 217 — right behind this office's east bookshelf! And someone pencilled in: PORTRAIT KNOWS THE COMBINATION.'
George Palm Sr., oil on canvas, stern as a heart attack. The brass plate reads: 'FOUNDED THIS PARADISE 6-9-1969.' ...That's a date you could open a safe with.

clickable_hotspots.label: Wall Safe
clickable_hotspots.locked_message: A sturdy wall safe with a numeric dial. Without the combination you'd be spinning it all night.

action_triggers.text: 6... 9... 1969 — CLUNK. Inside: a pre-drafted deed selling the Grand Palm to 'A. CRANE' for pennies, dated NEXT WEEK. He wasn't scaring buyers away for fun — he was clearing the field.

clickable_hotspots.label: East Bookshelf
clickable_hotspots.locked_message: A wall of mildewed ledgers and romance novels. Solid... probably.

action_triggers.text: Fred plants his feet, tugs the one clean book — 'GHOST STORIES, Vol. 2' — and muscles the whole bookshelf aside! Room 217 is real. 'And THAT, gang, is why you stretch first.' Head east... carefully.

clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: Velma taps the shelf: 'The blueprint says this moves... but it must weigh a ton.' Moving suspicious furniture is Freddy's department.

clickable_hotspots.label: Crane's Desk (locked drawer)
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: Crane's desk is drowning in paperwork — and the center drawer is LOCKED. Locked drawers are Daphne's favorite genre.

action_triggers.text: Daphne's hairpin pops the drawer in four seconds flat. Inside: overdue notices, foreclosure warnings, and a fresh newspaper — 'PALM HEIR RETURNS: GEORGE PALM III TO DECIDE RESORT'S FATE.' The heir is HERE? Then where is he?

clickable_hotspots.label: Globe Bar
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A fancy globe that's secretly a snack bar. Detecting hidden snacks is a joint Shaggy & Scooby operation.

action_triggers.text: Shaggy cracks the globe open — fancy peanuts, fresh crackers, ZERO dust. Scooby inhales the peanuts. 'Like, who restocks the SNACK GLOBE of an abandoned resort? A guy who still LIVES here, that's who!'

room_217.name: Room 217
room_217.description: The room that 'doesn't exist'. A phantom workshop — costume rack, sewing machine, paint-spattered workbench... and a young man tied to a chair, glaring at everyone.

clickable_hotspots.label: The Tied-Up Stranger
clickable_hotspots.missing_item_message: "MMMPH! MMFF-MMPH!!" He's trussed to the chair with knotted clothesline. You'll need something sharp to cut him free.

action_triggers.text: The workbench holds a megaphone rigged with a warbling reed — one whisper becomes the Phantom's pipe-organ WAIL. Shaggy tries it. Everyone regrets it. (Evidence bagged!)

clickable_hotspots.label: Costume Rack
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A rack of half-finished phantom sheets. Only one of us can read a hem line like a police report — get Daphne on the walkie.

action_triggers.text: Daphne works the rack like a sample sale: 'Machine-stitched hems, brand-new interfacing, and — a-HA — a receipt pinned inside: PALM COUNTY FABRIC, bought in BULK twelve days ago. Our ghost has a store account.'

clickable_hotspots.label: Sewing Machine
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A pedal sewing machine mid-project. Reverse-engineering machinery is Velma's happy place.

action_triggers.text: A pedal sewing machine threaded with glow-green thread. Velma touches the needle: 'Still WARM. Our phantom does his own tailoring — frugal, theatrical, and here within the hour.'

clickable_hotspots.label: Steamer Trunk
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A steamer trunk with rusted-shut latches. Percussive maintenance is Fred's love language.

action_triggers.text: Fred pops the trunk with one knee-drop. Inside: mothballs, spare linens... and CUT CHAINS that used to lock it shut. He grabs a clean old bedsheet. 'Trap supplies, gang. Trust me — a splash of that GLOWING PAINT and this sheet could star in its own haunting.'

clickable_hotspots.label: Room-Service Tray
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A room-service tray under a silver dome. Investigating unattended food is the founding charter of Shaggy & Scooby Incorporated.

action_triggers.text: Scooby lifts the cloche: half a club sandwich, STILL SOFT. Shaggy goes pale. 'Like, this is TODAY'S sandwich, man. The phantom had LUNCH here!' Scooby finishes the evidence. For safekeeping.

toolshed.name: Toolshed
toolshed.description: Inside the toolshed, lit by wobbling flashlight glow. Rakes, rust, and cobwebs — and that tantalizing GLINT under the floor grate. A pegboard shows chalk outlines where tools should hang.

clickable_hotspots.label: Floor Grate (something glints below)
clickable_hotspots.missing_item_message: The key is wedged deep under the grate. Your arm can't fit — you need something long, thin, and sticky to fish it out.

action_triggers.text: You lower the gummy stick through the grate... steady... GOT IT! A brass key stamped 'MAINTENANCE'. This must open the janitor's closet in the lobby.
An iron CRANK HANDLE hangs on the pegboard, square socket and all. Fred: 'This fits maintenance hatches. Pools have maintenance hatches. Gang — I have a plan.'

clickable_hotspots.label: Chalk Tool Outlines
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: Gus chalked an outline for every tool on this wall. Deducing what's MISSING — and why — is textbook Velma.

action_triggers.text: Velma scans the chalk outlines: 'BOLT CUTTERS — gone. PAINT SPRAYER — gone. Cutters open padlocks; sprayers paint phantoms. Somebody's been shopping in here after hours.' She taps her chin. 'And while we're inventorying — someone padlocked that little red toolbox. Daphne, that sounds like your kind of invitation.'

clickable_hotspots.label: Heavy Workbench
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: The workbench is bolted down... no, wait — just unbelievably HEAVY. Heavy is Fred's entire category.

action_triggers.text: Fred drags the workbench a foot — behind it, stuffed in the wall gap: an old LUMO-GLOW can converted into a stash tin, holding green-stained rags. 'Our phantom preps his act right here in Gus's shed. Bold.'

clickable_hotspots.label: Padlocked Toolbox
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A little red toolbox with a padlock the size of a grape. Adorable. Daphne eats grape-sized padlocks for breakfast.

action_triggers.text: Click. Daphne flips the toolbox open: oiled, organized, ALPHABETIZED... and one HORSESHOE MAGNET with a fresh price sticker. 'Somebody's been buying gear for this haunting. I'm borrowing the magnet — now we just need a good long LINE to swing it from.'

clickable_hotspots.label: Potting-Soil Sacks
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: Lumpy burlap sacks of potting soil. Poking suspicious lumps is a job for the gang's two professional lump-pokers.

action_triggers.text: Scooby pounces the lumpiest sack — POOF, soil everywhere — and drags out a rolled-up SLEEPING BAG stashed inside. Shaggy: 'Like, phantoms don't NAP, man! Somebody's been CAMPING in here!'

greenhouse.name: Greenhouse
greenhouse.description: The resort greenhouse: a glass cathedral of runaway plants. Moonlight filters through fogged panes onto moonflowers that only bloom for ghosts, apparently.

action_triggers.text: Half-buried in a planter: heavy GARDEN SHEARS, still sharp. Perfect for un-jungling that archway by the pool.

clickable_hotspots.label: Shattered Pot
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A shattered pot dusted with faintly glowing residue. Sample-swabbing is strictly a Velma operation.

action_triggers.text: A big pot lies shattered — the shards dusted with GLOWING GREEN residue. Velma swabs a sample: 'Someone stored paint cans in here before moving them. Our phantom rotates his hiding spots.'
A topiary grown wild into a lumpy, sheet-like shape. Shaggy points, screams, and climbs Scooby. Scooby climbs a shelf. The topiary continues to be a bush.

clickable_hotspots.label: Freshly Turned Soil
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A mound of freshly turned soil. Digging in questionable dirt is a certified two-expert job: one Shaggy, one Scooby.

action_triggers.text: Scooby digs like a furry backhoe — dirt EVERYWHERE — and unearths a wax-paper bundle: salami sandwich crusts and a soda bottle, still fizzy. Shaggy: 'Like, phantoms don't pack a LUNCH, man!'

clickable_hotspots.label: Tipped Stone Urn
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A tipped stone urn the size of Scooby. Righting furniture-adjacent boulders: that's a Fred call.

action_triggers.text: Fred deadlifts the urn upright — pressed into the soil beneath: a PERFECT size-12 boot print, diamond tread. 'Same tread as the pool prints, gang. Our phantom knocked this over running... and phantoms don't trip.'

clickable_hotspots.label: Snagged Thread
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: Something glints on the thorny vine by the west frame. Daphne notices details like that before the rest of us notice the greenhouse.

action_triggers.text: Daphne plucks a GLOW-GREEN thread off a thorn: 'Torn poly-cotton, machine-hemmed, snagged at shoulder height. Our phantom came through here in costume — and he's exactly Mr. Crane's height, by the way.'

pool_cabana.name: Pool Cabana
pool_cabana.description: The pool cabana: striped canvas gone grey, a lifeguard chair missing a leg, towel cubbies full of spiders' luggage. And humming away in the corner — a SCOOBY SNAX vending machine.

clickable_hotspots.label: Old Vending Machine
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: An ancient vending machine with one Scooby Snax box dangling on the spiral. It won't budge. Shaking loose snacks is a job for Shaggy and Scooby.

action_triggers.text: Shaggy and Scooby rattle the machine like maniacs! Out drops a box of Scooby Snax... and a pack of extra-sticky chewing gum wedged under the flap. 'Like, jackpot!'

clickable_hotspots.label: Lifeguard Logbook
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A mildewed logbook chained to the lifeguard stand. Water-damaged shorthand — Velma's department.

action_triggers.text: Velma deciphers the log: 'Final season — guest counts fell off a CLIFF the same week the Phantom first appeared. Almost like the haunting was... scheduled.'
Mildewed towels, one abandoned flip-flop, and a fossilized herring. Scooby eats the herring before anyone can object. 'Relicious.'

clickable_hotspots.label: Padlocked Staff Cabinet
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A padlocked STAFF cabinet built into the tiki bar. Rusty padlock, no key in sight — in other words, a Daphne problem.

action_triggers.text: Daphne's hairpin defeats the padlock before anyone finishes saying 'jinkies'. Inside: a staff blazer, DRY-CLEANED last week — ticket signed 'A.C.' 'An abandoned resort with a laundry day? Somebody still dresses like management.'

clickable_hotspots.label: Broken Lifeguard Stand
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: The lifeguard stand lists like a sinking ship — one leg snapped clean off. Sounds like a Fred-sized fix.

action_triggers.text: Fred heaves the lifeguard stand level and braces it with the broken leg. From the top: 'Gang — you can see EVERY window of the resort from up here, including the office. Perfect lookout for scheduling a haunting around witnesses.'

pump_room.name: Pump Room
pump_room.description: Under the pool: a cathedral of rusted pumps and dripping pipes. Glowing green footprints track through the dust, and somewhere in the dark, water goes drip... drip... drip.

navigation_links.label: Boiler Room
navigation_links.locked_message: The steel BOILER door is barred from the far side. The phantom clearly has his own way through... maybe you can fish that bar loose somehow.

clickable_hotspots.label: Maintenance Log
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A clipboard of pump readings and angry pencil. Technical scribbles — Velma will want this.

action_triggers.text: Velma reads the log: 'PUMPS SHUT DOWN — BY ORDER OF A. CRANE.' She frowns. 'Why drain the pool of a resort you claim you're trying to SELL? Unless empty pools scare buyers better.'
Under a tarp: a dozen empty LUMO-GLOW cans, brushes, and a phantom-sheet with a torn hem awaiting repairs. The Phantom's supply depot. Fred: 'He walks these tunnels to haunt the whole resort unseen.'

clickable_hotspots.label: Main Pump Valve
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: The main pump's handwheel is frozen solid, its spindle sheared to a bare square socket. Fred-grade torque required.
clickable_hotspots.missing_item_message: The handwheel spins uselessly — the spindle's sheared. Beside it: a square crank socket, the exact size of a certain iron handle...

action_triggers.text: Fred seats the crank and HEAVES. The pumps shudder, cough — and a black puddle gurgles away down a drain. Beneath it: MORE glowing prints and a LUMO-GLOW lid. 'These pumps work FINE, gang. The drained pool is a CHOICE. Somebody wanted paradise looking haunted.'

clickable_hotspots.label: Locked Breaker Cabinet
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: A steel breaker cabinet, latched tight, green light leaking from the seam. Cabinet locks are Daphne's cardio.

action_triggers.text: Daphne pops the latch: every resort breaker is OFF... except one unlabeled circuit, brand-new, wired straight past the meter and humming toward the boiler room. 'Our ghost doesn't pay an electric bill. Classy.'

clickable_hotspots.label: Oddly New Brickwork
clickable_hotspots.missing_item_message: Pipes, rust, and old brick... though one patch of bricks looks decades NEWER than the rest. Odd spot for renovations.

action_triggers.text: The marked map lines up — the X sits RIGHT HERE. The new bricks pull out like drawer handles: inside, Gus's missing BOLT CUTTERS and PAINT SPRAYER, wrapped in a monogrammed handkerchief. 'A.C.' The entire haunting kit, walled up in one hole.
A hand-drawn TUNNEL MAP, pinned at eye height: pool pumps → laundry → BOILER ROOM, marked in fresh green ink. So THAT's how the phantom crosses the resort without being seen.

clickable_hotspots.label: Steel Door — 'BOILER'
clickable_hotspots.missing_item_message: A steel door stenciled 'BOILER', barred from the other side. Cold green light seeps under a finger-high gap. Something long, thin, and MAGNETIC might fish that bar loose...

action_triggers.text: You feed the magnet under the door, walk it up the far side... CLICK — it grabs the latch bar. Everyone pulls. CLANG! The bar drops and the door groans open: a straight shot into the BOILER ROOM. The phantom's commute is now YOUR commute — head east.

clickable_hotspots.label: Glowing Footprints
clickable_hotspots.wrong_character_message: Glowing footprints track through the dust. Telling FRESH spooky paint from old spooky paint calls for a world-class nose — Scooby's, specifically.

action_triggers.text: Scooby snuffles along the prints... and his tail SHOOTS straight up. 'RESH! RERY RESH!' The phantom walked through MINUTES ago, heading for the boiler door. Shaggy climbs onto Scooby. Scooby climbs onto Shaggy. Somehow it works.
